title: To The Lady Elizabeth Harley, Since Marchioness Of Carmarthen, On A Column Of Her Drawing
content:
When future ages shall with wonder view
These glorious lines which Harley's daughter drew,
They shall confess that Britain could not raise
A fairer column to the father's praise.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Matthew Prior delivers a powerful performance in "To The Lady Elizabeth Harley, Since Marchioness Of Carmarthen, On A Column Of Her Drawing"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
